title: Sauvagesia imthurniana

intro: Sauvagesia imthurniana, commonly known as "Shapoya" or "Golondrina", is a plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sauvagesia imthurniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
